FT_IC1.evidence: Longitudinal data from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) Study, 21 U.S. sites, baseline ages 9-10; N=8292 at Year 2 follow-up (2018-2020).

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: longitudinal mediation model (GSEM) estimating associations between social media time (Year 2) and nicotine experimentation (Year 4) with smoking expectancies (Year 3) as mediators.

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed article published in The American Journal on Addictions (2026); no indication of duplicate or preprint of an already-included paper.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 hit: social media time (hours per day) is a phone/SM-specific variable with explicit numeric estimates paired with nicotine experimentation (substance use, an individual-trait outcome). Table 2: direct effect B=0.138 (95% CI 0.081-0.196, p<.001) in the positive-expectancy model and B=0.153 (95% CI 0.100-0.206, p<.001) in the negative-expectancy model.

FT_IC3.evidence: Multiple quantitative results reported: direct path coefficients (B), indirect effects, proportion mediated with 95% CIs and p-values from GSEM (Table 2, Figure 1).

FT_IC4.evidence: Inferential analysis (GSEM) performed on the SM-use to nicotine-experimentation relationship with numeric results throughout (Table 2).

FT_EC7_reason: Social media time is the IV (predictor) in all analyses; nicotine experimentation is the DV. EC7 requires SM to be DV-only, which is not the case here.
notes: Longitudinal ABCD data (Years 2-4); SM-as-IV prospective design with mediation through smoking expectancies. Straightforward INCLUDE.

FT_IC1.evidence: Cross-sectional data from Year 5 (2021-2023) of the ABCD Study (6.0 release); N=11,530; 21 demographically diverse U.S. sites, baseline ages 9-10.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: multivariable linear regression estimating association between dating app use (IV) and depression symptoms (DV) in ABCD Year 5 data.

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed article published in BMC Research Notes (2026); not identified as a duplicate or preprint of an already-included paper.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 hit: dating app use is a qualifying SM/phone variable (online dating apps explicitly listed in IC2 criteria). It appears as IV with its own coefficient paired with depression symptoms (mental health = individual-trait). Table 2: adjusted standardized beta ß=0.42 (95% CI 0.08-0.76, p=0.017). Sensitivity analyses (Appendix D) also report ß=0.44 (95% CI 0.03-0.85, p=0.035).

FT_IC3.evidence: Quantitative results reported: standardized beta coefficients, 95% CIs, and p-values from multivariable linear regression (Table 2); sensitivity and parallel analyses also provide numeric results.

FT_IC4.evidence: Inferential analysis (multivariable linear regression) performed on the dating-app-use to depression-symptoms relationship with numeric results (Table 2).

FT_EC7_reason: Design is cross-sectional (Year 5 only), not longitudinal baseline-predicts-later-wave. EC7 requires a longitudinal design, which is absent here.
notes: Cross-sectional ABCD Year 5 data; dating app use as IV, depression symptoms as DV. Clear INCLUDE.

FT_IC1.evidence: Data from the first wave of the ABCD study; large representative U.S. sample of 9-10 year olds across 21 sites; N=3847 after quality control.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: multiple machine learning regression approaches (LASSO, ridge, elastic net, linear regression) to estimate relative importance of 43 variables for BMI (%BMIp95) in ABCD baseline data.

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed article published in Psychological Medicine (2020); no indication of duplicate.

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: searched all results locations (Figs 1-3 LASSO/elastic-net coefficients, full-sample linear regression, sex-stratified models, Hispanic and Black non-Hispanic subsamples). The only screen-time variable in every analysis is defined as 'average minutes per week a child typically spends on a computer, cellphone, tablet, or other electronic device' -- a single multi-device aggregate with no per-device breakout anywhere in the paper. Step 2: this composite bundles computer, tablet, cellphone, and 'other electronic device' -- it is not exclusively phone/SM items. Per binding clarification, multi-device aggregated time fails Step 2. NOT_MET.

notes: Screen time defined explicitly as computer + cellphone + tablet + other electronic device aggregate; no per-modality (phone-only) breakout in any table, figure, or supplementary material. Excluded under FT-EC2.
